title: GPT-4.5 leaves ChatGPT tomorrow as OpenAI auto-migrates holdouts to GPT-5.x
subtitle: After a 30-day sunset that began on June 3, paid users who still had GPT-4.5 pinned will be silently switched to a default GPT-5.x model on June 27 — no action required.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: OpenAI will retire GPT-4.5 from ChatGPT on June 27, 2026, closing a 30-day sunset window that opened on June 3. Paid subscribers who had explicitly selected GPT-4.5 in their model picker will be moved to a default GPT-5.x model automatically, with no user action required, though anyone who deliberately pinned 4.5 in settings will notice the swap the next time they open a chat. The API version of GPT-4.5 was already deprecated back in July 2025, so this is strictly a consumer-product change.

The reasoning is straightforward and slightly clinical: GPT-4.5 has 'limited usage' inside ChatGPT now, and keeping it live costs serving capacity that OpenAI would rather route to GPT-5.4, GPT-5.5, and the new GPT-5.5-Cyber tier. Behind the scenes, OpenAI is also rolling out GPT-5.4 mini to Free and Go users via the 'Thinking' toggle, so the consumer experience continues to shift toward reasoning-by-default rather than picking between named legacy models.

What's interesting is how quiet this retirement is compared to the backlash earlier in 2026 when OpenAI deprecated GPT-4o, where users complained openly about losing what they felt was a more personable model. GPT-4.5 never developed that emotional attachment — it was always pitched as a stepping stone toward the GPT-5 family — and so it is leaving without ceremony. The contrast is itself a data point about which model generations have ended up mattering to end users versus to engineers.

A takeaway for learners: when you build anything that depends on a specific model name, treat the model as a perishable input. Pin versions in production, but plan for forced migrations every six to twelve months, and write your eval suite against behavior rather than against a model string. The product teams that handle deprecation cleanly are the ones that already had an automated way to re-benchmark on the replacement model the moment the sunset email arrived.

sources.title: ChatGPT — Release Notes (June 2026) — OpenAI Help Center
OpenAI Upgrades GPT-5.5 Instant and Confirms Retirement of o3 and GPT-4.5 Models — gHacks
OpenAI upgrades GPT-5.5, as it plans to retire legacy ChatGPT models — BleepingComputer
